subject: Re: denoised images with noise2noise
body:
Hi Dimitry,

I am enjoying the denoise feature on Warp as well, seeing the particles 
much easier than with bare eyes. For the purpose of picking particles in a 
different software (my particles are filaments), I would appreciate if 
denoised micrographs can be output along with the averages. Those images in 
my case will not be used for processing but only for picking particle 
coordinates.

Any idea for the stand-alone tool?

Best,
Peng

On Friday, September 21, 2018 at 9:03:18 AM UTC-7, Gabor Papai wrote:
>
> Hi Dimitry,
>
> Thanks for the explanation! 
> I will be happy to try the not pre-deconvolved images with the stand-alone 
> tool.
>
> Best,
> Gabor
>
> On Friday, September 21, 2018 at 3:21:29 PM UTC+2, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>
>> Hi Gabor,
>>
>> I'm afraid saving the denoised version has been intentionally left out of 
>> the current release. While the result is useful for visual interpretation, 
>> it's hard to say what kind of corruption the neural net introduces. Please 
>> note that the images are first deconvolved before the denoising, so the CTF 
>> is also very different. All downstream processing algorithms assume the 
>> data adhere to a physical model, and feeding them potentially corrupted 
>> data will lead to undefined behavior. I felt this would be too big a can of 
>> worms to open right now.
>>
>> I'm finalizing a stand-alone tool that lets you train the same network 
>> (and its 3D equivalent) on arbitrary 2D and 3D data. I'll let you know when 
>> it's done. If you really want to try using denoised 2D data for processing, 
>> I'd suggest using not pre-deconvolved images. They won't be as visually 
>> pleasing (see the example image attached), but closer to what the 
>> algorithms expect.
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>>
>> On Wednesday, September 5, 2018 at 9:50:16 AM UTC+2, Gabor Papai wrote:
>>>
>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>
>>> Is it possible to save the noise2noise denosied micrographs in warp?
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Gabor
>>>
>>
